name: Tonnarelli W/Caper Pesto
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tablespoons capers in salt, rinsed and patted dry
1 cup fresh flat leaf parsley leaves, loosely packed
1/4 cup fresh oregano leaves, loosely packed
1 tablespoon good quality red wine vinegar
1/2 pound imported dried tonnarelli, linguine or, spaghettini
1/2 cup Pecorino or Romano cheese, freshly grated
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill a stockpot with 6 quarts of water, cover and bring to a boil.
2. In a small saucepan, combine the olive oil and garlic; bring to a boil, cooking briskly until the garlic is golden. Remove from heat and let cool.
3. Transfer the garlic and oil to a blender or mini-processor. Add the tomato, capers, and pulse until chopped. Add the parsley and oregano and pulse until chunky, then add the vinegar.
4. Add 3 1/2 tablespoons of salt to the boiling water and add the tonnarelli. Cover partially until water just returns to a boil, then uncover, stir the pasta and cook until al dente. Drain the tonnarelli, reserving half cup of the pasta water.
5. Toss the pasta with the caper paste. If the tonnarelli is dry, add some of the pasta water, 1 tablespoon at a time. Garnish with cheese and serve immediately.
